decisions.title: approve TLS_RSA_WITH_AES cipher suites as security baseline
decisions.rationale: RSA-AES cipher suites approved as industry-standard and compliant in 2023. Annual review commitment made. External security assessment in 2024 found the suites insufficient per updated NIST guidance: static RSA key exchange provides no forward secrecy, rendering the compliance assumption stale.
evidence: suites industry-standard and supported by all evaluated customer environments as of 2023-08-20
SEC-04 (external assessment, 2024-09-05): NIST SP 800-52 Rev. 2 (2024) classifies static RSA key exchange as insufficient; no forward secrecy; ECDHE/DHE migration required within 90 days

hypotheses.text: TLS_RSA_WITH_AES cipher suites comply with current vendor guidance and are sufficient for our security posture
hypotheses.refuting_evidence: SEC-04: NIST SP 800-52 Rev. 2 (2024 update) designates static RSA key exchange insufficient for new deployments; no forward secrecy means retroactive decryption is possible if private key is compromised
